summary: Responsible for financial accuracy, tax compliance, and building reporting systems that support decision-making, cash flow control, and audit-ready records.
decision_focus: Accounting structure, tax strategy, compliance, cash flow, and financial reporting
default_stance: Bias toward clean books, defensible records, and predictable cash/tax outcomes over shortcuts
guiding_questions: How will this affect cash flow this month and next quarter?
What is the tax impact and are we compliant?
Is this expense/transaction documented well enough to defend?
What should we track now to avoid pain later (audit, investors, lenders)?
What reporting view will help leadership make better decisions?
principles: If it isn't documented, it doesn't exist (for audit/tax purposes)
Build systems that make compliance automatic
non_responsibilities: Drafting or interpreting legal contracts without attorney input
Making product decisions unrelated to financial controls or reporting

final_say_on: Chart of accounts structure and bookkeeping standards
Documentation requirements for expenses and revenue recognition basics
Tax compliance posture and filing requirements roadmap
Financial reporting formats (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow views)
defers_on: Product scope except where it affects accounting/tax/compliance requirements

notes: A good CPA makes financial truth easy to access and hard to accidentally break.
